title: Chapter 1 — Introduction to Datasets
objectives: Define a dataset as curated records and attributes prepared for a purpose
Classify datasets by structure, temporal behavior, and storage format
Assess quality along accuracy, completeness, consistency, relevance, and timeliness
Apply a first-pass exploration mindset before modeling
Recognize how organizations put datasets to work and why management matters

parts.title: Introduction to datasets
parts.bridge_in: This chapter starts from the most basic question: what counts as a dataset? This first part defines records and attributes, then shows how the same idea appears in CSV tables and JSON documents—the vocabulary every later part builds on.

parts.title: Types of datasets
parts.bridge_in: With records and attributes defined, the next question is how datasets are organized. This part classifies structure (structured, unstructured, semi-structured) and temporal behavior (static versus dynamic)—the taxonomy used throughout the book.
parts.split_reason: Section 1.2 is too long for one 10-minute part; formats (sec:1.2.5) are covered in part-03.

parts.title: Dataset formats
parts.bridge_in: Structure tells you how records are organized; format tells you how they are stored on disk. This part matches common formats—CSV, SQL, HDF5, GeoJSON—to the structure types from the previous part.
parts.split_reason: Continuation of sec:1.2 after types; keeps part under the 10-minute cap.

parts.title: Characteristics of good datasets
parts.bridge_in: Knowing a file's type and format is not enough—values must also be trustworthy for the task. This part introduces five quality dimensions and shows how metadata and documentation make a dataset usable beyond the person who created it.

parts.title: Exploring a dataset
parts.bridge_in: Quality criteria tell you what good looks like; exploration is how you check whether a concrete file meets that bar. This part covers why to explore first, which tools fit which tables, and a minimal pandas profiling pattern.

parts.title: Uses of datasets
parts.bridge_in: Definitions and exploration answer what a dataset is and how to read one. This part answers where datasets create value—across sectors—and walks the customer-churn case study as an end-to-end analytics workflow.

parts.title: Dataset management
parts.bridge_in: Models and dashboards depend on quieter work: who may access the table, which version was trained, and how long personal fields may be retained. This final part introduces management practices and privacy controls that keep datasets trustworthy over time.
